name: Albert Ayler
abstract: Albert Ayler (; July 13, 1936 – November 25, 1970) was an American avant-garde jazz saxophonist and composer. After early experience playing rhythm and blues and bebop, Ayler began recording music during the free jazz era of the 1960s.

works_created.t: Music Is the Healing Force of the Universe

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: Like Albert Ayler said, music is the healing force of the universe. I love that quote from him too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: he told me about an instance when he was working with I think Albert Ayler or somebody like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: I sat in and played with Albert Ayler the night that I came there, right away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Roswell Rudd
about_quotes.evidence: for instance here’s a wild little band, Albert Ayler on the tenor and soprano saxophones with Cecil Taylor on the piano, Sunny Murray on the drums and Louis Worrell on the bass, playing together in a coffee house in Greenwich Village in 1961 or something.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: When you want to talk about avant-garde, you’re talking about; Albert Ayler, Pharoah Saunders, or one phase of Archie Shepp, and Ornette Coleman, Don Cherry, Anthony Braxton, or people like that, the Chicago Art Ensemble, Joseph Jarman, and those people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roscoe Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: the way I chose the saxophone is what is your sound you know... But when I heard Albert you know I didn’t know what he was doing but I understood that he had an enormous sound on the instrument you know. So he won me over like right away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roscoe Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 23, 2023
